behavior_summary: Implements two long-running environmental 'storm' processes: a rain storm (U8P_STORM::func0080) and a fire storm (U8P_STORM::func021E). Each process marks its active state, produces periodic visual/sound effects, schedules the next tick via a spawned timer helper, and occasionally toggles its active effect off for a randomized pause before restarting.

Starter/stopper helpers exist for each storm: U8P_STORM::func0372 starts the rain process and sets a rainStorm marker; U8P_STORM::func0392 stops/cleans the rain process and clears the marker. Analogously, U8P_STORM::func03C2 starts the fire process and sets fireStorm, and U8P_STORM::func03E2 stops/cleans the fire process and clears fireStorm.

Visual coordination: when a global stormsActive flag is true, both storms request palette fades (via FadeToPalette) to flash the screen for lightning/fire effects. Each storm reads whether the other storm's active flag (rainStormActive or fireStormActive) is set to choose appropriate palette IDs for transitions, so rain/fire visuals are coordinated.

Rain behavior: U8P_STORM::func0080 spawns occasional LightningBolt effects, chooses randomized thunder SFX variants (urandom), and uses a spawned timer helper (spawn ->FREE::func1C19) to suspend until the next tick. It sets/clears rainStormActive to indicate transient rain activity during its loop.

Fire behavior: U8P_STORM::func021E attempts to create and hurl physical items (Item::legal_create followed by Item::hurl) as fire projectiles/debris, plays fire SFX, uses randomized offsets/velocities (urandom), and toggles fireStormActive similarly with longer random pauses when turned off.
notable_control_flow: Both storm processes run as loops that: set an 'Active' flag, optionally perform a visual palette flash if stormsActive, spawn a short-timer helper (spawn ->FREE::func1C19) computed from urandom + constant, suspend until the helper returns, perform effect work (spawn LightningBolt / create+hurl items / playSFX) with probabilistic branches, and occasionally clear the 'Active' flag to sleep for a long randomized interval before re-enabling.

Start helpers (U8P_STORM::func0372, U8P_STORM::func03C2) check a guard flag (rainStorm, fireStorm) to avoid double-spawning; they spawn the respective long-running process and set the guard flag to 1.

Stop helpers (U8P_STORM::func0392, U8P_STORM::func03E2) check the guard, optionally call FadeToPalette to restore/clear the screen when stormsActive, call FREE::resetRef to unregister the process reference (process type ids 0x0223/0x0224 and slot 0x01), and clear the guard flag.

Randomness (urandom) drives timing (short ticks and long sleep durations), chance-to-toggle behavior, SFX variant selection, and projectile position/velocity jitter—introducing non-deterministic, ambient storm behavior.

Palette fades are used both for momentary flashes (lightning/fire) and for transitions when a storm suspends/resumes; selection of palette id is conditional on whether the other storm is active, so transitions reflect combined environmental state.
open_questions: Exact numeric meanings of the palette IDs (0x03, 0x04, 0x00) are inferred as "rain flash" / "fire flash" / "clear" but the canonical mapping to art assets is not present in this script (confidence: medium).
Precise timing semantics and the role of the spawned helper ->FREE::func1C19: it appears to function as a sleep/timer helper but its internal behavior is external to this module (confidence: medium).

Exact item id/subtype semantics used by Item::legal_create (0x00FA with subtype 0x00A6 inferred) and whether created items are purely visual or interactable (damage, collision) — inference from Item::hurl suggests physical projectiles but not guaranteed (confidence: low).
The mapping of SFX ids/indices to concrete thunder or fire sounds is not listed here; the script chooses between variants via urandom but does not name them (confidence: low).

Whether stormsActive is globally toggled by other gameplay systems (weather controller, region triggers) — this script reads the flag but does not set it, so outside code governs global enabling (confidence: high for the separation, low for the controller specifics).

main_function.name: U8P_STORM::func0080
main_function.purpose: Run the rain-storm ambient loop: mark rainStormActive, produce palette flashes and lightning effects while storms are globally enabled, play randomized thunder SFX, schedule ticks with a spawned timer helper, and occasionally suspend rain activity for a randomized pause before resuming.
entry_conditions: Spawned by a starter helper (e.g., U8P_STORM::func0372) when a rain process is desired.
Operates only while globally-relevant flags (stormsActive) permit visual effects; behavior still runs but may mute visual/sound components depending on flags.

function_map.name: U8P_STORM::func0080
function_map.role: Rain-storm process loop: visual flashes, lightning spawn, thunder SFX, randomized toggles

function_map.name: U8P_STORM::func021E
function_map.role: Fire-storm process loop: visual flashes, create+hurl items as projectiles, fire SFX, randomized toggles

function_map.name: U8P_STORM::func0372
function_map.role: Rain-storm starter: spawn U8P_STORM::func0080 and set rainStorm guard

function_map.name: U8P_STORM::func0392
function_map.role: Rain-storm stopper/cleanup: optional palette fade, FREE::resetRef for process type 0x0223 slot 0x01, clear rainStorm guard

function_map.name: U8P_STORM::func03C2
function_map.role: Fire-storm starter: spawn U8P_STORM::func021E and set fireStorm guard

function_map.name: U8P_STORM::func03E2
function_map.role: Fire-storm stopper/cleanup: optional palette fade, FREE::resetRef for process type 0x0224 slot 0x01, clear fireStorm guard

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the rain-storm process and record its PID/reference (starter guard to prevent duplicate spawns)
Spawn the fire-storm process and record its PID/reference (starter guard to prevent duplicate spawns)
Indirect: U8P_STORM::func0392 resets/releases the running rain process reference (via FREE::resetRef) rather than directly calling the process; the linkage is lifecycle-management
Indirect: U8P_STORM::func03E2 resets/releases the running fire process reference (via FREE::resetRef) for lifecycle-management

external_calls.purpose: Request a palette fade/flash for lightning/rain visual effects; different palette ids chosen depending on other storm state
Schedule a timer/tick helper with a delay derived from urandom; used to suspend until next rain tick
Spawn a lightning visual/effect during rain ticks when stormsActive is set
Play randomized thunder SFX chosen by urandom during lightning events
Request a palette fade/flash for fire visual effects when stormsActive is set
Schedule a timer/tick helper with a delay derived from urandom; used to suspend until next fire tick
Attempt to create a projectile/debris item near the storm origin; success gates subsequent hurl and SFX
Launch a created item with randomized velocity offsets to produce moving fire projectiles
Clear/restore the screen palette when stopping the rain storm if stormsActive is set
Release/unregister the rain-storm process reference (process id 0x0223, slot 0x01) during cleanup
Clear/restore the screen palette when stopping the fire storm if stormsActive is set
Release/unregister the fire-storm process reference (process id 0x0224, slot 0x01) during cleanup
Select randomized delays and thunder SFX variants
Drive randomized timing, projectile position/velocity, and chance-to-toggle behavior

reads.name: stormsActive
reads.likely_effect: Gate whether visual palette flashes, lightning, and some toggle-related palette behavior occur; when false scripts suppress visual/sound effects

reads.name: fireStormActive
reads.likely_effect: Used by the rain-storm logic to choose which palette fade to apply (coordinate rain visuals with ongoing fire storm)

reads.name: rainStorm
reads.likely_effect: Guard used by starter helper to determine whether a rain process is already spawned (prevents duplicate spawns)

reads.name: fireStorm
reads.likely_effect: Guard used by fire starter helper to determine whether a fire process is already spawned (prevents duplicate spawns)

reads.name: rainStormActive
reads.likely_effect: Read by fire-storm helper to decide palette transition when fireStormActive toggles off (coordination)

writes.name: rainStormActive
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when U8P_STORM::func0080 enters its active loop and cleared when the loop probabilistically suspends rain effects; signals transient rain visual/sound activity

writes.name: fireStormActive
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when U8P_STORM::func021E enters its active loop and cleared when the loop probabilistically suspends fire effects; signals transient fire visual/sound activity

writes.name: rainStorm
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 by U8P_STORM::func0372 when the rain process is spawned; cleared by U8P_STORM::func0392 during cleanup (start/stop guard)

writes.name: fireStorm
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 by U8P_STORM::func03C2 when the fire process is spawned; cleared by U8P_STORM::func03E2 during cleanup (start/stop guard)

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements two long-running environmental 'storm' processes: a rain storm (U8P_STORM::func0080) and a fire storm (U8P_STORM::func021E). Each process marks its active state, produces periodic visual/sound effects, schedules the next tick via a spawned timer helper, and occasionally toggles its active effect off for a randomized pause before restarting.
